title: Alpha-L beta-2 integrin activation on MonoMac6 cells
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"clwdc","text":"In our study, we have discovered chemokine-like functions for the cytokine MIF such as induction of leukocyte arrest on endothelial cells. Cell adhesion requires the activation, i.e. by arrest chemokines, of integrins on the cell surface, which subsequently interact with adhesion molecules, such as VCAM-1 and ICAM-1. The latter interacts with the alpha-L beta-2 integrin. As MIF, like CXCL8, induced monocyte arrest on ICAM-1-expressing CHO cells1, we investigated the direct effect of MIF on integrin activation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":448,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Keep MonoMac6 cells (5×10 5 cells/mL) on ice in assay buffer until use.

Add one volume of assay buffer containing 100 ng/mL MIF (final concentration: 50 ng/mL) or canonical chemokine plus 2 mM Ca 2+ /Mg 2+ (final concentration: 1 mM Ca 2+ /Mg 2+ ) and incubate at 37 °C up to 30 min.
Negative control: incubate one sample (5×10 5 cells/mL) without stimulus for 30 min at 37 °C.
Positive control: incubate one sample (5×10 5 cells/mL) with 3 mM Mg 2+ and 1 mM EGTA
Withdraw samples at different times and add to one volume 4% PFA (final concentration: 2%)
Wash cells with PBS and incubate with the monoclonal antibody 327C (10 μg/mL) for 30 min.
Wash cells with PBS and incubate with anti-mouse-IgG FITC conjugate for another 30 min.
Measure the mean fluorescent intensity (MFI) of each sample via FACS as described by Shamri et al. 2 . The MFI corresponds with the integrin activation.
Work fast, because integrin activation already occurs within a minute after stimulus addition.
See for example Bernhagen et al . (Nat. Med. 2007).

MIF is a noncognate ligand of CXC chemokine receptors in inflammatory and atherogenic cell recruitment by J Bernhagen, R Krohn, H Lue, JL Gregory, A Zernecke, RR Koenen, M Dewor, I Georgiev, A Schober, L Leng, T Kooistra, G Fingerle-Rowson, P Ghezzi, R Kleemann, S R McColl, R Bucala, MJ Hickey & C Weber published in Nature Medicine on 28 Feb, 2007 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nm1567
